name: Bin CCD Image and Measure SNR Improvement
description: Spatial binning combines adjacent pixels to improve Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) at the cost of spatial resolution. You need to verify the theoretical √N SNR improvement by binning a real HST WFPC2 image (~/AstroImages/binning_analysis/ngc6652_555w.fits). Measure the Mean and StdDev of the background region specified in ~/AstroImages/binning_analysis/roi_region.txt. Create 2×2 and 4×4 binned versions of the image using the "Sum" method, saving them to ~/AstroImages/binning_analysis/output/ngc6652_bin2x2.fits and ~/AstroImages/binning_analysis/output/ngc6652_bin4x4.fits. For each binned image, measure the Mean and StdDev of the corresponding ROI (scaled by the binning factor). Calculate the SNR improvement for both binned images (ratio of binned SNR to original SNR) and generate a report at ~/AstroImages/binning_analysis/output/binning_report.txt containing exactly these keys (format: key: value, one per line): original_width, original_height, original_bg_mean, original_bg_std, bin2x2_width, bin2x2_height, bin2x2_bg_mean, bin2x2_bg_std, bin2x2_snr_improvement, bin4x4_width, bin4x4_height, bin4x4_bg_mean, bin4x4_bg_std, bin4x4_snr_improvement.
